decks.notes: The deck focuses on creating a powerful combo with Greasefang and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent with repeated triggers and massive creatures. It includes cards like Cache Grab and Multiversal Passage to search for key pieces while also providing disruption with Thoughtseize. The deck also includes additional support cards like Bitter Triumph, Esika's Chariot, and Witherbloom Command to further enhance the combo and control the board. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent before they can effectively respond.

This deck focuses on using Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can overwhelm opponents. It includes cards like Bitter Triumph and Thoughtseize for control, as well as Formidable Speaker and Raffine's Informant for card advantage. The deck also includes various lands to ensure mana consistency and access to different colors. Overall, the strategy revolves around setting up the combo pieces, controlling the board, and ultimately dominating the game with powerful creatures and combo synergies.

This Mono White Control deck focuses on utilizing a large number of enchantments and planeswalkers to control the board and give you advantage. With cards like Ajani, Outland Chaperone and Elspeth, Storm Slayer, you can generate tokens and removal spells to keep your opponent in check. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value with blink effects. The deck also benefits from mana ramp with cards like Nykthos, Shrine to Nyx and Castle Ardenvale to help you cast your powerful spells faster.

The main focus of this UW Control deck is to stall the game with counterspells and removal until it can establish a lock on the game with cards like The Wandering Emperor and No More Lies. It aims to control the board with cards like Supreme Verdict and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria while drawing cards and generating value with Yorion, Sky Nomad. The deck also utilizes lands like Floodfarm Verge and Field of Ruin to fix mana and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The ultimate goal is to outlast the opponent and win through incremental advantage.

The main concept of this deck revolves around creating a powerful combo using Greasefang and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards that support this combo such as Esika's Chariot, Darkbore Pathway, and Witherbloom Command. Additionally, there are cards like Boseiju, Who Endures and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's strategy and protect the combo pieces. The deck also includes a mix of removal spells, card draw, and ramp to ensure consistency and adaptability in different match-ups.

The main concept of this Gruul Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive creatures while dealing direct damage with efficient spells like Burst Lightning and Monstrous Rage. The deck focuses on building a strong board presence early in the game with creatures like Questing Druid, Emberheart Challenger, and Soul-Scar Mage, supported by burn spells like Reckless Rage. By combining efficient creatures with direct damage spells, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total and secure a victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of hand disruption, removal spells, and powerful creatures. By using cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Vanishing Verse, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain board control. Additionally, with creatures like Gifted Aetherborn and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, the deck can apply pressure while still controlling the game. The combination of disruption, removal, and threats allows the Orzhov Control deck to outlast opponents and dominate the game as it progresses.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear to deal direct damage to the opponent and control the board with spells like Reckless Rage and Burst Lightning. The deck also utilizes cards like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics to disrupt the opponent's strategy and maintain tempo. With a mix of efficient creatures and burn spells, this deck aims to apply relentless pressure on the opponent and win the game in the early turns before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on quickly getting Arclight Phoenix into the graveyard and then casting spells to bring it back to the battlefield for repeated attacks. The deck includes a mix of card draw, removal, and cheap spells to enable this strategy, with a variety of spells to support the aggressive playstyle. Additionally, the deck includes some cards for control and protection to help keep the opponent's board in check while building towards the win condition of overwhelming with Arclight Phoenix.

The main focus of this deck is to use Greasefang Parhelion in combination with other cards like Formidable Speaker and Esika's Chariot to create a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm the opponent. By using cards like Bitter Triumph and Cache Grab to control the board and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's strategy, this deck aims to establish board presence and ultimately defeat the opponent with the synergies created by its key cards. Additionally, the deck includes a mix of lands and support cards to enhance its consistency and versatility in different game situations.

This deck focuses on using the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes various lands to support the combo, along with creatures like Iron-Shield Elf and Fleeting Spirit to protect and enhance the combo. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push provide disruption and removal, while cards like Lively Dirge and Vanishing Verse offer additional control options. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and outpace the opponent for victory.

Utilizing a combination of card draw, removal spells, and counter magic, this Izzet Control deck focuses on maintaining board control and out-valuing opponents. By consistently drawing cards and disrupting the opponent's game plan with bounce spells and counterspells, the deck aims to establish a dominant position on the battlefield while slowly chipping away at the opponent's resources. With efficient threats like Cori-Steel Cutter and Otawara, Soaring City, the deck can quickly close out games once it has gained control of the game.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the combination of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy. The deck focuses on generating value through sacrificing creatures to Greasefang and utilizing Monument to Endurance to protect them. Additionally, cards like Fleeting Spirit and Iron-Shield Elf provide additional support and protection for the combo. The deck also includes removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to create a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent with its combo potential.

The main idea of this deck is to assemble the combo pieces of Greasefang Parhelion, Parhelion II, and Greasefang, Okiba Boss to create a powerful synergy that can overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards that help in searching for key pieces, protecting the combo, and disrupting the opponent's strategy. The combination of creatures, spells, and lands work together to generate value and create a winning board state. By efficiently utilizing these cards and interactions, the deck aims to achieve victory through a well-coordinated combo strategy.

The deck focuses on generating value through the combination of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II, utilizing cards like Formidable Speaker and Overlord of the Balemurk to support the combo. The deck also includes disruption elements like Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command to control the game and protect the combo. The mana base is designed to support the color requirements of the cards in the deck while also including utility lands like Lush Portico and Boseiju, Who Endures. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful synergies.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, using cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate powerful synergies. It also includes disruption and removal spells like Thoughtseize and Bitter Triumph to control the game until the combo can be assembled. The deck aims to quickly ramp up its board presence and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creatures and powerful artifacts. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around setting up its combo pieces and dominating the battlefield with a mix of creatures and spells.
